name: Rosemary-Olive Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp fresh rosemary, minced or 1 tsp dried rosemary
4 oil-packed dried tomatoes, drained and cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Place dough in a bowl. Add 3 tbsp olive oil and the minced or dried rosemary. Season generously with pepper.
3. Knead the dough until all ingredients are combined.
4. Roll the dough out on a lightly floured work surface into a 9x6-inch rectangle.
5. Transfer the dough to a baking sheet. Flatten and press it into a 12x9-inch rectangle.
6. Rub 1 tbsp olive oil over the dough.
7. Sprinkle 1/2 cup parmesan over; press gently into the dough.
8. Arrange olives, tomatoes, and garlic on top of the bread.
9. Sprinkle the remaining Parmesan to lightly cover the surface.
10. Drizzle the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil over the focaccia.
11. Top with rosemary sprigs.
12. Continue baking until the cheese melts and the bread is cooked through, about 5 minutes.
13. Cut into squares or wedges and serve.
